title: 24 Receiving Incoming Wires
body_text:
24	Receiving Incoming Wires
24.1	Overview of Receiving Incoming Wires
Incoming wires from financial institutions that donГўВҖВҷt participate in Wires upload to Wires approximately every ten minutes. Incoming wires sent from a financial institution that also participates in Wires immediately display in Pending Incoming. Incoming wires are available on business days (Monday to Friday, excluding statutory holidays).
You must accept pending incoming wires and credit to the receiving customerГўВҖВҷs account or return the incoming wire to the sending financial institution as appropriate. Follow your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs policies and procedures for accepting or returning incoming wires.
You must comply with all regulatory requirements. This includes, but isnГўВҖВҷt limited to:
Scanning wires against sanction lists
Record keeping
Identification of the beneficiary
PEP/HIO determination
FINTRAC reporting
Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part.
To accept or return pending incoming wires, the Wires User must have the MTS ГўВҖВңUpdate Incoming TransfersГўВҖВқ permission and have a 2-step security token assigned to their Client Centre ID. See Section 8.6, Creating a Wires User in MTS.
Note: 	Wires users only see wires that correspond to the branches assigned to the user in MTS. See Chapter 9, MTS Branch Management for Wires.
24.2	Routing Instructions for Incoming Wires
Central 1 can receive incoming wires in multiple currencies. Incoming wires must include routing instructions identifying the beneficiary financial institution and the correspondent financial institution, if applicable. The sending financial institution must use the correct routing information or the incoming wire may be delayed, converted at an unfavourable exchange rate, or returned. Specifically, wires received with missing identifiers will be returned to the sending financial institution.
Note:	IBANs havenГўВҖВҷt been assigned to Canadian financial institutions.
Your customers and other financial institutions may request your routing information to provide to the sender.
Routing instructions are available as forms for the most common currencies. This includes incoming wires sent in Canadian dollars, US dollars, Euros, Pounds Sterling (GBP), Australian dollars, Swiss Francs, Japanese Yen, Mexican Pesos, Polish Zlotys, and New Zealand dollars.
Important
: 	Use the correct form for the wire currency.
Complete all fields on the appropriate form. Ensure that you enter your route and transit as the beneficiary financial institution. Missing or incorrect information may result in the incoming wire being delayed or returned.
Canadian Dollar Domestic Incoming Wires
ГўВҖВ“ Complete the Routing Instructions ГўВҖВ“ Canadian Dollar (CAD) Domestic Incoming Wires, Form 2605. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Routing Instructions ГўВҖВ“ Canadian Dollar (CAD) Domestic Incoming Wires, Form 2605
US Dollar Incoming Wires
ГўВҖВ“ Complete the Routing Instructions ГўВҖВ“ US Dollar (USD) Incoming Wires, Form 2606. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Routing Instructions ГўВҖВ“ US Dollar (USD) Incoming Wires, Form 2606
Canadian Dollar International Incoming Wires
ГўВҖВ“ Complete the Routing Instructions ГўВҖВ“ Canadian Dollar (CAD) International Incoming Wires, Form 2607. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Routing Instructions ГўВҖВ“ Canadian Dollar (CAD) International Incoming Wires, Form 2607
Euro Incoming Wires
ГўВҖВ“ Complete the Routing Instructions ГўВҖВ“ Euro (EUR) Incoming Wires, Form 3710. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Routing Instructions ГўВҖВ“ Euro (EUR) Incoming Wires, Form 3710
Australian Dollar Incoming Wires
ГўВҖВ“ Complete the Routing Instructions ГўВҖВ“ Australian Dollar (AUD) Incoming Wires, Form 3771. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Routing Instructions ГўВҖВ“ Australian Dollar (AUD) Incoming Wires, Form 3771
Swiss Franc Incoming Wires
ГўВҖВ“ Complete the Routing Instructions ГўВҖВ“ Swiss Franc (CHF) Incoming Wires, Form 3772. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: Routing Instructions ГўВҖВ“ Swiss Franc (CHF) Incoming Wires, Form 3772
British Pound Sterling Incoming Wires
ГўВҖВ“ Complete the Routing Instructions ГўВҖВ“ British Pound Sterling (GBP) Incoming Wires, Form 3773. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Routing Instructions ГўВҖВ“ British Pound Sterling (GBP) Incoming Wires, Form 3773
Japanese Yen Incoming Wires
ГўВҖВ“ Complete the Routing Instructions ГўВҖВ“ Japanese Yen (JPY) Incoming Wires, Form 3774. See Figure 8.
Figure 8: Routing Instructions ГўВҖВ“ Japanese Yen (JPY) Incoming Wires, Form 3774
Mexican Peso Incoming Wire
ГўВҖВ“ Complete the Routing Instructions ГўВҖВ“ Mexican Peso (MXN) Incoming Wires, Form 3775. See Figure 9.
Figure 9: Routing Instructions ГўВҖВ“ Mexican Peso (MXN) Incoming Wires, Form 3775
Polish Zloty Incoming Wires
ГўВҖВ“ Complete the Routing Instructions ГўВҖВ“ Polish Zloty (PLN) Incoming Wire, Form 3776. See Figure 10.
Figure 10: Routing Instructions ГўВҖВ“ Polish Zloty (PLN) Incoming Wires, Form 3776
New Zealand Dollar Incoming Wire
ГўВҖВ“ Complete the Routing Instructions ГўВҖВ“ New Zealand Dollar (NZD) Incoming Wires, Form 9352. See Figure 11.
Figure 11: Routing Instructions ГўВҖВ“ New Zealand Dollar (NZD) Incoming Wires, Form 9352

24.3	Scanning Incoming Wires Against Sanctions Lists
Central 1 scans all incoming wires against government-provided sanctions lists, however your financial institution is responsible for identifying and taking subsequent action regarding wires to a sanctioned individual or entity. This includes, but isnГўВҖВҷt limited to, the following:
Identifying the beneficiary.
Scanning all incoming wires against sanctions lists to identify matches of individual name, entity or organization name, and financial institution.
Handling incoming wires if the sender or beneficiaryГўВҖВҷs name matches an entry on the sanctions lists.
Determining record keeping and FINTRAC reporting requirements.
If Central 1 identifies a possible match, the status of the wire in the Wires application changes to ГўВҖВңFrozenГўВҖВқ. Central 1 will confirm if the incoming wire requires further investigation within one business day. If Central 1 determines that further investigation isnГўВҖВҷt required, the status of the incoming wire changes from ГўВҖВңFrozenГўВҖВқ to ГўВҖВңPendingГўВҖВқ in the Wires application.
Note:	The ГўВҖВңFrozenГўВҖВқ status also displays during the scanning process and may take several minutes to complete.
If Central 1 confirms that an incoming wire matches an entry on the sanctions lists or requires further investigation, the wire status changes from ГўВҖВңFrozenГўВҖВқ to ГўВҖВңUnder InvestigationГўВҖВқ in the Wires application. Central 1ГўВҖВҷs Compliance Department works with your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs corporate security department and any required government agency to determine the outcome of the funds. Submit a FINTRAC Suspicious Transaction Report if the situation requires this.
Incoming wires that have a status of ГўВҖВңFrozenГўВҖВқ or ГўВҖВңUnder InvestigationГўВҖВқ can be viewed in the Wires application but canГўВҖВҷt be accepted or returned.
Note:	Incoming wires arenГўВҖВҷt credited to your financial institution until the ГўВҖВңFrozenГўВҖВқ or ГўВҖВңUnder InvestigationГўВҖВқ status is removed. Funds are placed in a holding account at Central 1 while the incoming wire is investigated.
Follow your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs policies and procedures for incoming wires that are frozen or under investigation. Your financial institution must establish a policy to ensure that staff donГўВҖВҷt alert the individual when an incoming or outgoing wire is frozen or under investigation. If asked, advise the individual that the transfer is pending but donГўВҖВҷt divulge any information that may compromise an investigation.
PCMLTFA
prohibits the disclosure of information on transactions that will be or have been reported, regardless of whether a criminal investigation has begun. Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part for additional information.
24.4	Reviewing Incoming Wires
Authorized users must review incoming wires to determine if you should accept or return the wire. You must comply with all regulatory requirements pertaining to incoming wires. This includes, but isnГўВҖВҷt limited to, scanning wires against sanction lists, record keeping, identification of the beneficiary, PEP/HIO determination, and FINTRAC reporting. Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part.
To review an incoming wire:
Log in to Wires as described in Chapter 10, Logging in to Wires.
Select
Pending Incoming
. See Figure 12.
Figure 12: Select ГўВҖВңPending IncomingГўВҖВқ
Enter the code thatГўВҖВҷs generated on your registered 2-step security token, then select
Done
. See Figure 13.
Figure 13: Entering a 2-Step Security Code
Select the reference number of the appropriate wire. See Figure 14.
Figure 14: Select the Wire to Accept
Tip
:	Use
Show only wires you can accept
to filter the Pending Wires to display wire transfers that you are authorized to accept. See Figure 15.
Figure 15: Select ГўВҖВңShow only wires you can acceptГўВҖВқ
View the wire details as described in Section 19.2, Viewing Wires and Wire Details. Determine if you should accept or return the wire and identify any regulatory and/or reporting requirements.
Note:	If an incoming wire has a cross-border status of ГўВҖВңVerify ManuallyГўВҖВқ, your financial institution or AML vendor must manually review the ГўВҖВңSending Financial InstitutionГўВҖВқ information to determine the sending financial institutionГўВҖВҷs country.
Confirm that the name and account number of the beneficiary (your customer) match the name and account number in your records.
Important
:	Further to CPA By-law No. 7, section 49, Identification of Payee, if a financial institution relies on the account number to credit the beneficiary and doesnГўВҖВҷt notice a discrepancy between the name on the account and the name in the payment message, it has satisfied its obligations to the beneficiary. However, if you notice that the name on the transfer differs from the name on the account, donГўВҖВҷt credit the beneficiary. The finality of payment may not apply. Either issue a trace request or return the incoming wire to the sending financial institution.
Report International Incoming Wires of $10,000 (Canadian equivalent) or more to FINTRAC
. Submit the FINTRAC EFT Report to FINTRAC within five business days of receipt of the incoming wire. Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part for additional information.
Note:	You can add additional information for FINTRAC Reporting on incoming wires from a non-Wires participant financial institution if applicable. See Section 24.6, Entering Additional Information for FINTRAC Reporting.
Submit a FINTRAC Suspicious Transaction Report
if there are reasonable grounds to believe that the incoming wire is related to a money laundering or terrorist financing offence. Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part for additional information.
Important
:	Your financial institution canГўВҖВҷt disclose to an individual that it will submit a FINTRAC Suspicious Transaction Report.
Submit a FINTRAC Terrorist Property Report
if your financial institution knows or believes it has an incoming wire in its possession or control thatГўВҖВҷs owned or controlled by or on behalf of a terrorist, terrorist group, or listed person. Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part for additional information.
Make a PEP/HIO Determination
w
ithin 14 days of receipt of a very large international wire if the beneficiary is an individual. A PEP determination isnГўВҖВҷt required if the beneficiary is an entity. Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part for additional information.
Complete a Foreign Exchange Transaction Ticket
if an incoming wire is exchanged into a currency other than that in which it was received. Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part for additional information.
You must accept the wire as described in Section 24.5, Accepting Incoming Wires, or return the wire as described in Section 24.7, Returning Incoming Wires, as appropriate.
24.5	Accepting Incoming Wires
Follow your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs policies and procedures to determine if you should accept or return an incoming wire. This section includes procedures for accepting an incoming wire and accepting an incoming ГўВҖВңreturnГўВҖВқ type wire. See Section 24.7, Returning Incoming Wires, if you canГўВҖВҷt accept the incoming wire (e.g., it was sent to your financial institution in error). You canГўВҖВҷt return a ГўВҖВңreturnГўВҖВқ type wire.
Accepting an Incoming Wire
To accept an incoming wire:
Locate the appropriate wire as described in Section 24.4, Reviewing Incoming Wires.
Select
Accept Wire
, then select
Yes
when prompted.
Figure 16: Select ГўВҖВңAccept WireГўВҖВқ
Important
:	The Wires application isnГўВҖВҷt connected to your banking system. Follow your internal procedures to settle with the sender.
Confirmation that the wire is successfully accepted displays and the status of the wire changes to ГўВҖВңAcceptedГўВҖВқ. See Figure 18.
Figure 18: Accepted Wire Confirmation
Note:	You can add additional information for FINTRAC Reporting on incoming wires from a non-Wires participant financial institution if applicable. See Section 24.6, Entering Additional Information for FINTRAC Reporting.
Note:	Verify if the wire contains structured remittance information. This information must be provided to your customer. See Section 19.2, Viewing Wires and Wire Details, for more information.
Accepting a ГўВҖВңReturnГўВҖВқ Type Incoming Wire
If the incoming wire type displays as ГўВҖВңReturnГўВҖВқ, the receiving financial institution returned a wire that was created by your financial institution. You canГўВҖВҷt return a ГўВҖВңReturnГўВҖВқ type incoming wire.
To Accept a ГўВҖВңReturnГўВҖВқ type incoming wire:
Review the details of the incoming wire as described in Section 24.4, Reviewing Incoming Wires, and accept the wire as previously described.
Determine the reason for return. Return codes and/or reasons display in the Sender FI to Receiver FI Information section on the Pending Incoming Wires ГўВҖВ“ Details page and Received Wires ГўВҖВ“ Details page.
Tip:	Use ISO 20022 Return Code Definitions, Form 9610, to find a return code definition.
ГўВҲВ’
If a data entry error was made, create an outgoing wire with the correct information.
ГўВҲВ’
If no data entry error was made, contact the sender and verify the details of the original outgoing wire. Create a new outgoing wire if the sender wants to resend the wire or credit the senderГўВҖВҷs account for the appropriate amount.
Note:	The financial institution that receives a ГўВҖВңReturnГўВҖВқ type incoming wire is responsible for all associated fees. This includes, but isnГўВҖВҷt limited to, service charges from Central 1ГўВҖВҷs correspondent bank, service charges from the financial institution that returned the transfer, and fees for traces. The financial institution that receives the ГўВҖВңReturnГўВҖВқ type incoming wire is also responsible for foreign exchange losses. For foreign exchange wires returned by financial institutions that donГўВҖВҷt participate in Wires, the FX rate effective the date the return wire is received is used.
Related Information
ISO 20022 Return Code Definitions,
Form 9610
24.6	Entering Additional Information for FINTRAC Reporting
You can enter additional information for FINTRAC reporting purposes for wires received from a non-Wires participant financial institution. This function is available according to how your financial institution customized the Wires application. Specifically, itГўВҖВҷs either available for all incoming wires or only for those that the system detects may need to be reported to FINTRAC. See Section 4.2, Description of Wires Customizable Elements. Additional information for FINTRAC reporting must be entered by 5:00 pm local time, the business day after the wire is received.
You must comply with all regulatory requirements. Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part.
To enter additional information that may be reported to FINTRAC:
Locate the appropriate wire as described in Section 24.4, Reviewing Incoming Wires.
Select
View Personal Information
. See Figure 19.
Figure 19: Select ГўВҖВңView Personal InformationГўВҖВқ
Enter the additional personal information, then select
Save
.
Note:	The cut-off time displays at the top of the page. See Figure 20. If the wire isnГўВҖВҷt accepted before the cut-off time, a message displays informing the user that the personal information can no longer be edited. See Figure 21.
Figure 20: Enter Additional Information for FINTRAC Reporting
Figure 21: You Can No Longer Edit Personal Information Message
Select
Reload
to refresh the list of pending incoming wires. See Figure 22.
Figure 22: Select ГўВҖВңReloadГўВҖВқ to Refresh the List of Pending Incoming Wires
24.7	Returning Incoming Wires
Follow your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs policies and procedures to determine if you should accept or return an incoming wire. Wires that canГўВҖВҷt be accepted must be returned to the sending financial institution. A returned wire is pre-populated with data from the original incoming wire.
Important
:	Incoming wires must be reported to FINTRAC even if returned to the sending financial institution. Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part for additional information.
An incoming wire may be returned for various reasons including, but not limited to, the following:
Beneficiary Account Number Mismatch
Beneficiary Account Number Incorrect
Beneficiary Account Number Missing
Beneficiary Account Closed
Beneficiary Deceased
Beneficiary Name Incorrect
Beneficiary Name Missing
Correspondent Bank Incorrect
Financial Institution Incorrect
Intermediary Incorrect
Only Receive CAD Wires
Regulatory Concern
Transit Incorrect
Note:	DonГўВҖВҷt select the ГўВҖВңCreate ReturnГўВҖВқ option in the Wires menu to create a return wire. You must return a wire from the original pending incoming wire. If you select this option, a message displays advising you to go to the Pending Incoming or Received Wires page to return a wire.
To return an incoming wire:
Locate the appropriate wire as described in Section 24.4, Reviewing Incoming Wires.
Select
Return Wire
. See Figure 23.
Figure 23: Select ГўВҖВңReturn WireГўВҖВқ
The following information pre-populates on the Create Return Wire page. See Figure 24.
ГўВ‘
Sending Financial Institution
ГўВҖВ“ The name and branch of the financial institution sending the return wire. The information is derived from the BeneficiaryГўВҖВҷs Financial Institution section of the original incoming wire. For example, if Easy Savings Credit Union was the beneficiary of the incoming wire, they are the sender of the return wire.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
BeneficiaryГўВҖВҷs Financial Institution
ГўВҖВ“ The Swift/Transit number and address of the financial institution receiving the return wire.
ГўВ‘Вў
Wire Amount
ГўВҖВ“ The wire amount details:
ГўВҲВ’
Amount: The amount that will be returned. The field is editable.
ГўВҲВ’
Currency: The return currency.
ГўВҲВ’
Sender Total: The settlement value of the return wire.
Note:	The beneficiary financial institution isnГўВҖВҷt responsible for any fees.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Additional
Information
ГўВҖВ“ The reference number from the original incoming wire.
Note:	For Foreign exchange wires returned to financial institutions that donГўВҖВҷt participate in Wires, the FX rate effective the date that the return wire is sent is used.
Figure 24: Create Return Wire Page
Complete the Return Reason Information section. See Figure 25. You can enter a narrative reason for return or select a return reason code.
Narrative Return Reason
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the reason for returning the wire in the Additional Reason Information field. The Selected Return Reason field defaults to ГўВҖВңNARRГўВҖВқ to indicate a narrative reason for return.
Return Reason Code
ГўВҖВ“ Select
Find Return Reason Code
to search for and select an ISO20022 return reason code.
Figure 25: Create Return Wire Page
To search for a return reason code:
Enter a key word in the Description field or leave the field blank to view all available codes, then select
Search
. See Figure 26.
Note: 	The Additional Reason Information field doesnГўВҖВҷt display when a return reason code is used.
Figure 26: Searching for a Return Reason Code
Select the appropriate return reason code. Select
Yes
to confirm the selected code. See Figure 27 and Figure 28.
Figure 28: Selecting a Return Reason Code
Figure 29: Confirming the Return Reason Code
Select
Next
to proceed with the return. See Figure 29.
Figure 29: Selecting ГўВҖВңNextГўВҖВқ
Note:	An error message displays if you donГўВҖВҷt enter a reason for return. See Figure 30.
Figure 30: Return Reason Error Message
Select
Create Return Wire Transfer
. See Figure 31. Alternatively, select
Edit
to make changes or select
Cancel
to cancel the creation of the return transfer. Select
Print Confirmation
to print a confirmation of the return.
Figure 31: Send Return Wire Confirmation
A message displays confirming the return wire was successfully created and the number of approvals required. See Figure 32.
Figure 32: Wire Successfully Created Message
The return wire moves to Pending Outgoing. You must approve the return wire before itГўВҖВҷs sent. See Chapter 22, Approving Outgoing Wires.
Note:	The wireГўВҖВҷs status displays ГўВҖВңReturnedГўВҖВқ in the Wire application once itГўВҖВҷs fully approved.
END
